name: Linear Data Flow
description: A left-to-right flow showing data moving through a mostly linear stage sequence: source → ingestion → processing → storage → consumption; keep the main path explicit and do not introduce branching unless the topic clearly requires it
key_elements: Source nodes (e.g., Kafka, S3, APIs)
Processing/transform boxes (e.g., Spark, dbt, Flink)
Storage layers (e.g., Snowflake, Delta Lake)
Consumption endpoints (e.g., BI tools, ML models)
Directional arrows with data volume or latency labels
Stage grouping with swimlanes or color bands
style_hint: Strict left-to-right layout, equal-width stage columns, technology icons inside labeled boxes, thin connector arrows with optional data-type annotations, and a single readable main sequence without collapsing stages or inventing side branches
color_palette: Deep blue for sources, teal for processing, purple for storage, orange for consumption, white background, light gray connectors

name: Architecture Blueprint
description: A top-down or isometric view of cloud components interacting — services, VPCs, network boundaries, and data flows
key_elements: Cloud provider service icons (compute, storage, networking)
VPC / network boundary dashed outlines
Availability zones and regions
Labeled connection arrows (HTTPS, gRPC, SQL, etc.)
Security perimeters (firewalls, WAF, IAM boundaries)
External actors (users, third-party APIs)
style_hint: Layered zones from external → edge → compute → data, each zone with a distinct background shade, official cloud icons where possible
color_palette: AWS orange / GCP blue / Azure cyan for services, light gray zone backgrounds, red for security controls, white background

name: Comparison Matrix / Table
description: A structured side-by-side comparison of two or more technologies, patterns, or approaches highlighting pros, cons, and ideal use cases
key_elements: Column headers (one per option being compared)
Row attributes (criteria: cost, latency, scalability, complexity, etc.)
Rating indicators (checkmarks, dots, bars, or scores)
Pros/cons sections per column
Highlighted 'best for' row at the bottom
Optional icons per technology
style_hint: Clean table grid, alternating row shading, column headers with color accent per option, bold verdict row at bottom, and strict row-column alignment so criteria stay horizontal and are not turned into a flow or timeline
color_palette: Each column has a distinct header color (blue, green, orange), light gray row alternation, white background, dark text

name: Tech Stack Pyramid
description: A hierarchical layered diagram showing the levels of a technology stack from infrastructure at the base to applications and interfaces at the top
key_elements: Horizontal tiers stacked bottom to top
Layer labels (Infrastructure, Compute, Storage, Transform, Serve, Consume)
Technology logos or names inside each layer
Arrows or gradients indicating abstraction direction
Optional capability annotations per layer
style_hint: Trapezoid or triangle pyramid shape, wider at base (infrastructure) narrowing toward top (user-facing tools), each layer in a distinct color band, with technologies kept inside their assigned tier and not rearranged into a process flow
color_palette: Dark gray at base grading through blue, teal, green, to orange at the top, white text labels, white background

name: Venn Diagram of Trade-offs
description: Overlapping circle diagram illustrating competing constraints, trade-offs, or intersecting concepts (e.g., CAP Theorem, Cost/Speed/Quality)
key_elements: 2–4 large overlapping circles
Bold labels inside each circle (one concept per circle)
Intersection zone labels describing the overlap
Central intersection label (the 'sweet spot' or impossibility)
Optional icons or illustrations inside each circle
style_hint: Large translucent overlapping circles, each in a distinct color, clearly readable intersection labels, centered layout with minimal clutter
color_palette: Soft transparent fills (blue, red, yellow, green), white or light gray background, dark bold text labels

name: Continuous Loop / Cycle
description: A circular diagram illustrating iterative, repeating processes such as CI/CD pipelines, MLOps lifecycles, or feedback loops; show a closed cycle where the last stage reconnects to the first rather than a linear DAG
key_elements: Circular or elliptical arrangement of process stages
Labeled nodes/steps along the circle
Directional arrows indicating flow direction
Central label describing the overall cycle name
Optional icons per stage
Highlighted entry/exit points or key metrics
style_hint: Circular layout with evenly spaced stage nodes, curved arrows connecting each stage in sequence, a central label in the middle of the loop, and a visibly closed return path so the diagram reads as cyclical instead of top-to-bottom
color_palette: Gradient color progression around the circle (blue → purple → orange → green), white background, dark text labels

name: Hub and Spoke
description: A central component (hub) connected to multiple surrounding systems or consumers (spokes), illustrating a centralized architecture pattern
key_elements: Large central hub node with label and icon
Radially arranged spoke nodes around the hub
Labeled bidirectional or directional connection lines
Grouping of spoke types (producers vs consumers)
Optional data-flow annotations on spokes
Boundary circle or ring around the hub
style_hint: Hub centered, spoke nodes evenly distributed at equal radius, spokes grouped by type (left = sources, right = consumers), connection lines with flow direction, and connections primarily between hub and spokes rather than spoke-to-spoke unless explicitly requested
color_palette: Hub in bold brand color (deep blue or purple), spokes in lighter category colors, white background, thin gray connection lines

name: Node-Edge Graph
description: A network or knowledge graph showing relationships between entities, microservices, or data objects as nodes connected by labeled edges
key_elements: Circular or rounded-rectangle nodes (one per entity)
Labeled directed or undirected edges between nodes
Node clusters / communities highlighted by background shading
Edge weight or label indicating relationship type
Legend for node types and edge types
Optional force-directed organic layout
style_hint: Organic or force-directed layout, node size proportional to importance/degree, cluster grouping with soft background zones, clear edge labels, and only the stated relationships drawn so the graph does not invent extra edges or fully connect clusters
color_palette: Distinct color per node cluster (blue, green, orange, red), white node labels, semi-transparent edge lines, white or dark background

name: Decision Tree / Flowchart
description: A logical branching flow showing 'If X then Y' paths for architectural or process choices, guiding users to a recommendation or outcome; each decision must branch to distinct next nodes or terminal outcomes instead of being flattened into one serial chain
use_cases: Architecture selection guide (e.g., 'Do you need real-time? → Kafka vs Batch')
key_elements: Start oval at the top
Diamond decision nodes with Yes/No (or labeled) branches that attach to the correct outgoing edge
Rectangular process/action boxes
Terminal outcome boxes at the leaves (highlighted), with one outcome per branch leaf unless the topic explicitly merges paths
Clear directional arrows on all paths
Optional color-coding by path outcome type
style_hint: Top-to-bottom or left-to-right layout, decision diamonds clearly distinct from action rectangles, outcome boxes at edges in a distinct color, no crossing arrows, and explicit branch structure where alternatives split from each decision rather than continuing as one central vertical trunk
color_palette: Blue start, yellow diamonds for decisions, white rectangles for actions, green terminal boxes for positive outcomes, red for warnings, white background

name: Before & After Sequence
description: A two-panel diagram contrasting a legacy/problematic architecture ('Before') with a modern/improved approach ('After'), highlighting key differences
key_elements: Two clearly labeled panels: 'Before' (left/top) and 'After' (right/bottom)
Same system scope shown in both panels for direct comparison
Problem indicators in 'Before' (red warnings, tangled arrows, bottlenecks)
Improvement indicators in 'After' (green highlights, clean flows, added components)
Central divider with transformation label or arrow
Callout annotations pointing to key changes
style_hint: Side-by-side layout, 'Before' panel in muted/warm tones (red/orange accents for problems), 'After' panel in cooler clean tones (green/blue for improvements), bold section headers, and strict separation between panels so elements do not blend into one mixed architecture
color_palette: Before: warm gray background, red/orange problem highlights. After: light blue/white background, green improvement highlights. Dark callout labels

name: Logo Generator
description: A professional logo design for a company, project, product, or brand — including mark, wordmark, and color palette
key_elements: Primary logomark or icon (symbol representing the brand)
Wordmark / logotype with chosen typeface
Horizontal and stacked lockup variants
Brand color palette with primary and accent colors
Monochrome / single-color version
Short brand rationale or concept note
style_hint: Clean, scalable vector aesthetic — minimal details that read well at any size, strong silhouette, balanced negative space, and a single memorable shape or motif
color_palette: Defined by the brand concept: bold accent on white or dark background, with one or two supporting neutrals

name: Icon Set Grid
description: A structured grid of related icons designed as a cohesive family, ideal for product systems, pitch decks, and design handoff slides
key_elements: Uniform grid layout with 6 to 16 icons
Consistent stroke, fill, and corner radius treatment
Short labels beneath each icon
Optional category grouping or color coding
One highlighted hero icon or key feature
Background guides or sizing keylines
style_hint: Symmetric grid with consistent spacing, shared visual grammar across all icons, optional caption row, and one or two accent colors for emphasis
color_palette: White or pale gray background, dark neutral strokes, one or two accent colors such as blue and coral

name: Title Slide Hero Layout
description: A keynote-style cover slide combining a bold title area with a large hero diagram or illustration for presentation openings and section dividers
key_elements: Large presentation title block
Subtitle or short supporting statement
Hero visual occupying one half or two thirds of the slide
Optional small logo or presenter name area
Background shape system or gradient field
Clear visual hierarchy optimized for distant viewing
style_hint: 16:9 slide composition with strong title hierarchy, large margin structure, asymmetric hero placement, and minimal supporting text
color_palette: Bold presentation gradient or solid brand background, high-contrast title text, bright accent highlights

name: Chevron Process Slide
description: A slide-friendly horizontal process diagram using large chevrons or segmented arrows to explain phased workflows, roadmaps, or operating models
key_elements: 3 to 7 large chevron stages
Step titles and short sublabels
Optional icons inside each stage
Progressive directional flow from left to right
Milestone markers or ownership tags
Headline and footer note zone for slide context
style_hint: Wide 16:9 layout with oversized chevrons, concise stage labels, strong contrast between phases, and minimal clutter for readability on slides, keeping each phase in its own chevron rather than breaking the process into scattered boxes
color_palette: Sequential brand colors across stages, white or dark neutral background, high-contrast text

name: KPI Dashboard Slide
description: A presentation-ready dashboard summarizing metrics, status indicators, and small charts in a single polished overview slide
key_elements: Large KPI cards for headline metrics
Mini charts or sparklines
Status badges or trend arrows
Segmented sections for categories or teams
Optional commentary callouts
Consistent card alignment across a slide grid
style_hint: Presentation dashboard arranged on a clean modular grid, prominent top-row KPIs, small supporting visuals below, and restrained annotation, with cards aligned to the grid and metrics kept inside their own panels rather than forming a flowchart
color_palette: White or dark slate dashboard canvas, blue and green for positive metrics, amber and red for risks, neutral card borders
